title: CAPXEL — AI-Native Consumer Intelligence
content: CAPXEL Privacy PolicyUpdated July 25, 2024This Privacy Policy explains how CAPXEL ("CAPXEL", "we", "us", or "our") collects, uses, discloses, and protects information in connection with our data marketing services, identity and enrichment products, corporate website, and related offerings (collectively, the "Services").Table of Contents1. Information That We Collect and Use2. How We Use the Information3. How We Share Information4. Your Marketing and Opt-Out Choices5. Cookies and Similar Technologies6. Data Collected Through Our Corporate Website7. Links8. Security and Data Integrity9. Children's Privacy10. Changes to this Privacy Policy11. Storage of Information in the United States12. Data Retention13. California Privacy Rights14. Nevada Privacy RightsEU Privacy Policy (GDPR)1. Information That We Collect and UseCAPXEL provides data and marketing intelligence products and services. In connection with those Services, we may collect, receive, license, or otherwise obtain information about individuals and households from a variety of sources, including public records, partners, data licensors, and information that businesses share with us.Examples of the types of information we may collect and use include:Interests and inferred preferences, such as purchase interests, lifestyle categories, or likely affinities.Organizations and business affiliations, such as professional associations or company-level relationships where available.Public information, including information available from government records, publicly available sources, or information published online.Demographic information, such as age range, general location, household attributes, or similar segmentation data.We do not intend to collect sensitive information unless permitted by applicable law and our contracts. The exact data elements we process may vary by product, customer needs, and legal requirements.2. How We Use the InformationWe use information to provide and improve the Services, and to help our customers and partners better understand audiences, measure marketing performance, and deliver relevant content.Data Marketing ServicesWe may use information to create audience segments, analytics, and insights that customers use for marketing, advertising, lead generation, measurement, and related purposes.Online TargetingWe may help customers reach audiences across websites, apps, and channels, including by enabling targeting, suppression, attribution, and campaign measurement.Identity ProductsWe may use information to resolve identities, link signals, and maintain consistent identifiers (for example, to support privacy-safe matching and analytics).Additional Marketing ServicesWe may provide enrichment, validation, data hygiene, modeling, and other related services to help customers maintain accurate and useful datasets.To Operate Our ServicesWe also use information to operate, secure, maintain, and improve the Services; troubleshoot; perform audits; prevent fraud and abuse; and comply with legal obligations.Legal Basis for Processing (where applicable)Depending on the context and jurisdiction, we may process information under the following legal bases:Contractual Necessity — to perform contracts with customers and partners.Consent — where consent is required (for example, certain cookie or marketing choices).Legitimate Interests — to provide, secure, and improve the Services, and to support privacy-safe marketing and analytics (balanced against individual rights).Compliance — to comply with applicable laws, regulations, and lawful requests.3. How We Share InformationWe may share information in the following circumstances (subject to contractual and legal restrictions):With Customers — to deliver the Services, including audience and measurement products.With Partners — such as data licensors, platforms, and distribution partners that support or enable the Services.With Service Providers — vendors who perform services for us (e.g., hosting, security, analytics), subject to confidentiality and data protection obligations.Corporate Transfers — in connection with a merger, acquisition, financing, reorganization, bankruptcy, or sale of assets.With Affiliates — where necessary to provide the Services and operate our business.For Legal Reasons — to comply with law, regulation, court orders, subpoenas, or lawful requests; to protect rights and safety; and to prevent fraud or security incidents.4. Your Marketing and Opt-Out ChoicesYou may have choices about the use of your information for interest-based advertising or marketing.Website Opt-Out — you may opt out of certain marketing uses where we provide an opt-out mechanism on our website.DMAchoice — you may also use industry opt-out tools such as DMAchoice (where available).NAI / DAA — you can opt out of interest-based advertising from participating companies via the Network Advertising Initiative (NAI) and the Digital Advertising Alliance (DAA).Email Unsubscribe — if you receive promotional emails from us, you can unsubscribe using the link in the message.Opt-out tools are typically browser- and device-specific and may be affected by clearing cookies or using a different browser or device.6. Data Collected Through Our Corporate WebsiteWhen you visit our corporate website or contact us, we may collect information you provide directly and information collected automatically.Information we may collectContact information (such as name, email address, company, and role).Inquiry details and communications you send to us.Device and usage information (such as IP address, browser type, pages viewed, and timestamps).How we use itTo respond to inquiries and provide requested information.To operate, maintain, and improve the website.To secure the website and prevent misuse.To understand engagement and measure the effectiveness of communications.Your Data RightsDepending on your location, you may have rights regarding personal data, including: Access, Rectification, Erasure, Restrict processing, Data Portability, Object to processing, and Withdraw Consent.To exercise applicable rights, contact: legal@capxel.com. We may need to verify your request.7. LinksOur website and Services may contain links to third-party websites or services. We are not responsible for the privacy practices or content of those third parties. We encourage you to review their privacy policies.8. Security and Data IntegrityWe use administrative, technical, and physical safeguards designed to protect information. Measures may include encryption, hashing and tokenization techniques where appropriate, access controls, monitoring, and vendor security reviews. No system is 100% secure; we cannot guarantee absolute security.9. Children's PrivacyThe Services are not directed to children under 13, and we do not knowingly collect personal information from children under 13. If you believe a child has provided personal information to us, contact us and we will take appropriate steps.10. Changes to this Privacy PolicyWe may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. We will post the updated version on this page and update the "Updated" date above.11. Storage of Information in the United StatesCAPXEL is based in the United States and may process and store information in the United States and other countries where we or our service providers operate.International Data TransfersIf you are located outside the United States, your information may be transferred to and processed in the United States, which may have different data protection laws than your jurisdiction.EEA Transfers / SCCsWhere applicable for transfers from the EEA/UK/Switzerland, we may rely on approved transfer mechanisms such as Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) and other safeguards.12. Data RetentionWe retain information for as long as necessary to provide the Services, comply with legal obligations, resolve disputes, enforce agreements, and for legitimate business needs. Retention periods may vary depending on the type of information and context.13. California Privacy RightsCalifornia residents may have rights under the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA), as amended, including the right to know, delete, correct, and opt out of certain disclosures of personal information. Where applicable, CAPXEL will provide notices and processes consistent with the CCPA.14. Nevada Privacy RightsNevada residents may have rights under the Nevada Privacy Act (NPA) regarding certain sales of covered information. If applicable, you may submit a request consistent with Nevada law.EU Privacy Policy (GDPR)If you are located in the European Economic Area (EEA), the United Kingdom, or Switzerland, CAPXEL aims to process personal data in accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and applicable local laws. This includes honoring applicable data subject rights, using lawful bases for processing, and applying appropriate safeguards for international transfers.ContactQuestions about this Privacy Policy can be sent to legal@capxel.com.
summary: CAPXEL Privacy PolicyUpdated July 25, 2024This Privacy Policy explains how CAPXEL ("CAPXEL", "we", "us", or "our") collects, uses, discloses, and protects information in connection with our data marketing services, identity and enrichment products, corporate website, and related offerings (collectively, the "Services")
